game_name: Joe & Mac 2: Lost in the Tropics

element_introduction_order.element: Boss encounters (Stegosaurus, Pteranodon, Triceratops, Elasmosaurus, Tyrannosaurus)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Climactic challenge requiring skill accumulation; final level requires defeating all bosses sequentially
Traversal aid and safety mechanic providing relief

element_introduction_order.element: Environmental hazards (jungle obstacles, snowy terrain, swamp, volcano)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Spatial challenge escalation tied to world progression
Narrative progression gate; earned through boss mastery
Ultimate test; defeats yield crown and game completion

typical_relief_triggers: Level completion and progression to next biome
Stone wheel collection breaks action pace
Overhead map freeroam provides pacing control between levels
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating tension through biome progression; relief provided by player agency (level select via map) and friendly creature mechanics; final level spike through mandatory boss sequence

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Overhead freeroam map allows optional level selection and replay for stone wheel currency farming; suggests optional content exists

difficulty_curve.early_game: Easy to moderate (introductory valley level establishes baseline mechanics with Neanderthals and basic platforming)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate (jungle, snowy mountain, swamp introduce environmental complexity and dinosaur variety; friendly creatures provide safety valves)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Hard (volcano setting; final level forces sequential boss gauntlet with Gork two-phase finale after all previous bosses)
notable_spikes: Boss encounters at each level conclusion (difficulty jump from traversal to combat)
Final level boss gauntlet (5 boss rematches + Gork transformation)
Volcano environment (highest environmental hazard density implied by final placement)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 6 levels introduces distinct environment (valley, jungle, snowy mountain, swamp, volcano, final) with escalating hazards; each concludes with unique dinosaur boss encounter. Progression earns rainbow stone.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Valley (Level 1) → Jungle (Level 2) → Snowy Mountain (Level 3) → Swamp (Level 4) → Volcano (Level 5) → Final (Level 6)

notable_level_patterns.description: Friendly rideable creatures (Pteranodon, Styracosaurus, Plesiosaurus) interrupt danger rhythm and provide safe traversal windows, signaling player-controlled pacing zones.
Level 6 demands sequential defeat of all previous bosses before confronting Gork, requiring mastery of all accumulated combat patterns. Gork's transformation adds mechanical surprise.
Overhead freeroam map permits level selection and replay, breaking linear pacing. Stone wheel currency incentivizes optional replays, extending engagement.
